from.title: Pentecost Tongues
from.body: # Pentecost Tongues ## What happens Fifty days after Passover, the Spirit descends on the gathered disciples with sound of wind and tongues of fire; they proclaim God's mighty works in the many languages of the nations gathered in Jerusalem. ## Typological significance Babel reversed — scattered tongues regathered around one gospel. Sinai's fire on the mountain becomes Spirit-fire on each believer…

to.title: Cloven Tongues Pentecost
to.body: # Cloven Tongues at Pentecost ## The appearance Tongues "as of fire" appear, divided, and rest on each of the gathered believers, who then speak in the languages of the diaspora pilgrims gathered in Jerusalem. ## Mode / context Pentecost morning, Jerusalem, c. AD 30. ## Significance Babel reversed — divided tongues now communicate the mighty works of God to every nation; the Spirit anoints each in…

from.title: Absalom Hung On Tree
from.body: # Absalom Hangs in a Tree → Cursed One on the Tree ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 18:9-15 Absalom's hair catches in the branches of a great oak; he hangs between heaven and earth while his mule rides on. Joab pierces him with three darts. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 3:13: Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree. - Deuteronomy 21:22-23: A hanged man is cursed by God. - John 19:34: Pierced in the side. ## …

to.title: Absalom Rebellion
to.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

from.title: Revelation 16 Bowls Of Wrath
from.body: # Revelation 16: Bowls of Wrath ## NT Text — Rev 16 Seven bowls of wrath are poured — sores, blood, scorching sun, darkness, Euphrates dried. Egyptian plagues climax in cosmic recapitulation. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 7-12** — Egyptian plagues. - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of wrath drained. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to nations. ## Shared structure - Bowls poured - Seven completes - Plague-…

to.title: Revelation 8 Trumpets
to.body: # Revelation 8: Seven Trumpets ## NT Text — Rev 8:6-13 Seven trumpets sound. Hail and fire mixed with blood, sea turned to blood, water made bitter — Egyptian plagues recapitulated against the world. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 7-12** — Egyptian plagues. - **Joel 2:1-11** — Trumpet alarm and locust army. - **Josh 6** — Jericho trumpets. ## Shared structure - Trumpet-summons - Cosmic plagues - Egypt…

from.title: Samson Solo Victory In Death
from.body: # Samson's Death Brings Greater Victory → Christ Triumphs by Dying ## OT Story — Judges 16:23-31 Blinded, mocked, between two pillars, Samson prays once more. He pushes; the temple collapses; he kills more in his death than in his life. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:14-15: Through death he destroyed the one who has the power of death. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them…

to.title: Samson Strength
to.body: # Samson's Strength ## What happens A Nazirite from birth, Samson performs feats of impossible strength against the Philistines, and in death — blinded and bound — pulls down their temple, killing more than in life. ## Typological significance A flawed savior whose final act is voluntary death that destroys the enemy — a shadow of the crucified Christ whose apparent defeat is His decisive triumph.…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
to.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

from.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
from.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

to.title: Words From Cross Into Your Hands
to.body: # Word from Cross: Into Your Hands I Commit ## NT Text — Luke 23:46 Jesus dies praying Psalm 31:5 — a Davidic prayer of trust. Stephen later echoes it. Death is recast as commendation. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Eccl 12:7** — Spirit returns to God. - **Acts 7:59** — Stephen echoes this prayer. ## Shared structure - Spirit-commendation - Trust in Father…

from.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
from.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

to.title: Joseph In The Pit
to.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

from.title: Apocalypse Of Weeks Enoch
from.body: # Apocalypse of Weeks → Periodized History and Final Age ## OT Story — 1 Enoch 93; 91:11-17 History is divided into ten 'weeks'; the seventh sees apostasy and the chosen of the eternal plant; later weeks bring judgment and new creation. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 24:14: This gospel will be preached throughout the world, and then the end. - 2 Peter 3:13: New heavens and new earth. - Galatians 4:4:…

to.title: Ezekiel Apocrypha Themes
to.body: # 4 Ezra / Apocalyptic Hope → New Creation Theology Backdrop ## OT Story — 2 Esdras (4 Ezra) 7 4 Ezra wrestles with theodicy, exile, and resurrection — featuring the Messiah's reign, new age dawning, and consolation of the righteous. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:18-25: Sufferings of this present time vs. coming glory. - 2 Corinthians 4:17: Light affliction working eternal glory. - Revelation 21:1-…

from.title: Barabbas Substitution
from.body: # Barabbas Substitution ## NT Text — Matt 27:15-26 A guilty 'son of the father' is released while the innocent Son of the Father is condemned. Substitution is dramatized publicly. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:7-10** — Two goats — one released, one slain. - **Isa 53:5** — Wounded for our transgressions. - **Exod 12:13** — When I see the blood I will pass over. ## Shared structure - Two figures cont…

to.title: Trial Before Pilate Innocent Verdict
to.body: # Trial Before Pilate: Innocent Verdict ## NT Text — John 18:28-19:16 Pilate repeatedly declares Jesus innocent yet hands him over. The pattern matches the Servant unjustly condemned and the Passover lamb inspected and found without blemish. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:8-9** — Cut off though no violence in him. - **Exod 12:5** — Lamb without blemish. - **Ps 2:1-2** — Rulers gather against the LOR…

from.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
from.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

to.title: Words From Cross Father Forgive
to.body: # Word from Cross: Father, Forgive Them ## NT Text — Luke 23:34 Jesus intercedes for his crucifiers, echoing the Servant who 'made intercession for transgressors.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **Num 14:19-20** — Moses intercedes for Israel. - **Gen 50:17** — Joseph forgives brothers. ## Shared structure - Intercession amid suffering - Forgiveness offered - …

from.title: hagios
from.body: # hagios (ἅγιος) — holy ## Semantic field Holy, set apart, consecrated. Rarely used in pagan Greek — its NT content is shaped by LXX renderings of kadosh. ## Theological weight God is *the* Hagios. The Spirit is "Pneuma Hagion." Believers are "hagioi" — saints, the consecrated people. Holiness defines both God's character and the church's calling. ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew kadosh. ## Em…

to.title: kadosh
to.body: # kadosh (קָדוֹשׁ) — holy ## Semantic field Set apart, distinct, belonging exclusively to God. Holiness is first a description of God's own utterly other being, derivatively applied to whatever is consecrated to Him. ## Theological weight The trisagion ("Holy, Holy, Holy" — Isa 6:3) names God's defining attribute. Israel is called to be kadosh because YHWH is kadosh. ## Septuagint mapping Rendered…

from.title: Glory On Sinai
from.body: # Glory on Sinai ## The appearance The mountain is wrapped in thick cloud, thunder, lightning, and trumpet blast; YHWH descends in fire, the mountain quakes, and Moses ascends into the cloud where the glory dwells; later YHWH passes by, proclaiming his name. ## Mode / context Covenant-making at Sinai; the constitutive theophany of Israel. ## Significance The encounter that founds the covenant and …

to.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
to.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire ## The appearance YHWH goes before Israel by day in a pillar of cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire, to lead them; the same pillar stands between Israel and the Egyptians at the sea. ## Mode / context Throughout the wilderness wanderings; visible, mobile, and protective. ## Significance Continuous divine presence guiding the people; ancestor of the shekhinah glory th…

from.title: Flood Baptism
from.body: # Flood / Ark → Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 6-9 A righteous remnant is preserved in a wooden vessel as God's judgment washes the world clean. Noah's family passes through the waters of death and emerges into a new creation, greeted by a covenant rainbow. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 3:20-21: "Baptism, which corresponds to this, now saves you" — through-water salvation in Christ's resu…

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: Psalm 110 Priest King
from.body: # Psalm 110 / 'Sit at My Right Hand' → Priest-King Forever ## OT Story — Psalm 110 'The LORD says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool… You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 22:41-46: Jesus' messianic riddle from this psalm. - Hebrews 5:6; 7:1-28: Christ's eternal priesthood after Melchizedek. - Acts 2:34-36: Pete…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
from.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire → God Tabernacling With Us ## OT Story — Exodus 13:21-22; 40:34-38 The LORD goes before Israel as a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night. The pillar fills the finished tabernacle. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled among us; we beheld his glory. - Matthew 17:5: A bright cloud overshadows; 'this is my beloved Son.' - Revelation 21:23:…

to.title: Tabernacle Pattern From Heaven
to.body: # Tabernacle Built to Heavenly Pattern → Heavenly Sanctuary ## OT Story — Exodus 25:9; 25:40 Moses is to build the tabernacle 'according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.' The earthly is a copy of the heavenly. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 8:5: They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. - Hebrews 9:23-24: Christ entered the true sanctuary, of which the earthly is a copy. - Revelat…

from.title: Colossians 2 Buried And Raised With Christ
from.body: # Colossians 2: Buried and Raised with Christ ## NT Text — Col 2:11-13 Believers are circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, buried with Christ in baptism, raised with him through faith. Old self stripped, new self received. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 30:6** — Circumcise your heart. - **Jer 4:4** — Circumcise yourselves to the LORD. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and new spirit. ## Share…

to.title: Colossians 2 Nailed To Cross Bond
to.body: # Colossians 2: Bond Nailed to the Cross ## NT Text — Col 2:14-15 The certificate of debt with its legal demands is nailed to the cross, and principalities are publicly disarmed. The cross is a triumphal procession. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:5** — Chastisement of our peace upon him. - **Exod 14** — Pharaoh's host overthrown. - **Ps 68:18** — Ascended on high, led captives. ## Shared structure -…

from.title: Daniel 7 Four Beasts
from.body: # Daniel 7 — Four Beasts ## The oracle Daniel sees four great beasts arising from the chaotic sea — lion, bear, leopard, and a dreadful fourth — representing four kingdoms; the little horn of the fourth speaks great things and wages war against the saints. ## Date / context Vision in the first year of Belshazzar, c. 553 BC. ## Fulfillment Traditionally identified with Babylon, Medo-Persia, Greece,…

to.title: Daniel 8 Ram And Goat
to.body: # Daniel 8 — Ram and Goat ## The oracle Daniel sees a two-horned ram (Medo-Persia) overthrown by a swift one-horned goat from the west (Greece); the great horn breaks and is replaced by four, from one of which a little horn arises and defiles the sanctuary. ## Date / context Third year of Belshazzar, c. 551 BC. ## Fulfillment Fulfilled in Alexander the Great, the diadochi, and Antiochus IV Epiphan…

from.title: Healing Of Blind Bartimaeus
from.body: # Healing of Blind Bartimaeus ## What happens Outside Jericho, the blind beggar Bartimaeus cries out 'Son of David, have mercy.' Jesus calls him; he throws off his cloak and is given sight, then follows Jesus on the way. ## Typological significance Confession of Davidic messiahship paired with restored sight — the messianic age of Isaiah 35 has arrived. The cloak cast off and the following 'on the…

to.title: Healing Of Man Born Blind
to.body: # Healing of the Man Born Blind ## What happens Jesus mixes clay with saliva, anoints the eyes of a man blind from birth, and sends him to wash in the pool of Siloam ('Sent'). He returns seeing, and confesses Jesus as Lord. ## Typological significance Re-creation imagery: clay from the ground, divine breath, Sabbath-day formation of a new humanity. The progressive illumination of the man contrasts…

from.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

to.title: Sermon On Mount New Sinai
to.body: # Sermon on the Mount as New Sinai ## NT Text — Matt 5-7 Jesus ascends a mountain, sits down, and authoritatively teaches his disciples. Matthew structures the scene to evoke Moses at Sinai — but the new lawgiver speaks not 'thus says the LORD' but 'I say to you.' ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19-20** — Moses on Sinai delivers the Decalogue. - **Deut 30:11-14** — Word near you — internalized Torah an…

from.title: Ezekiel 36 New Heart
from.body: # Ezekiel 36 / New Heart, New Spirit → Born of Water and Spirit ## OT Story — Ezekiel 36:25-27 'I will sprinkle clean water on you… A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you. I will remove the heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 3:5-8: Born of water and Spirit. - Titus 3:5: Washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit. - 2 Cor…

to.title: Ezekiel 47 River From Temple
to.body: # River from the Temple → River of Life From the Throne ## OT Story — Ezekiel 47:1-12 Water flows from under the temple threshold — ankle, knee, waist, swimming-deep. It heals the Dead Sea. Trees on its banks bear fruit each month; their leaves heal. ## NT Fulfillment - John 7:37-39: Rivers of living water from the believer — Spirit. - Revelation 22:1-2: River of the water of life from the throne,…

from.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

to.title: Wedding Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wedding Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Rev 19 From Adam's awakening to Eve to Cana's water-turned-wine to the marriage supper of the Lamb, weddings mark new-creation thresholds. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:22-24** — First wedding. - **Song 3:11** — Day of Solomon's wedding. - **Isa 62:5** — Like a bridegroom rejoices. ## Shared structure - Anticipation - Feast - Joy - Covenant initiat…

from.title: Firstborn
from.body: # Symbol: Firstborn The firstborn carries inheritance, blessing, and consecration to God in the OT — and is the most precious thing the parent can lose or give up. ## Hebrew - **בְּכוֹר** (*bekhor*) — firstborn. ## Greek - **πρωτότοκος** (*prōtotokos*) — firstborn. Used of Christ: "firstborn of all creation" (Col 1:15), "firstborn from the dead" (Col 1:18, Rev 1:5), "firstborn among many brothers"…

to.title: Israel As Firstborn
to.body: # Israel My Firstborn → Christ the Firstborn ## OT Story — Exodus 4:22-23 The LORD's word to Pharaoh: 'Israel is my firstborn son. Let my son go that he may serve me. If you refuse, I will kill your firstborn son.' ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:29: Firstborn among many brothers. - Colossians 1:15-18: Firstborn of all creation; firstborn from the dead. - Hebrews 12:23: The assembly of the firstborn …

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
to.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

from.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

to.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

from.title: John Baptist As Elijah
from.body: # John the Baptist as Elijah ## NT Text — Matt 11:14; 17:11-13; Luke 1:17 Jesus identifies John the Baptist as the Elijah who was to come. John wears Elijah's hair garment and leather belt, preaches in the wilderness, and confronts a wicked king and queen — the pattern is unmistakable. ## OT Antecedents - **Mal 4:5-6** — Elijah sent before the great day of the Lord. - **2 Kgs 1:8** — Elijah's garm…

to.title: Revelation 11 Two Witnesses
to.body: # Revelation 11: Two Witnesses ## NT Text — Rev 11:3-13 Two witnesses prophesy in sackcloth, with power to shut up the heavens and turn water to blood — Elijah and Moses echoes. Slain, then raised after three and a half days. ## OT Antecedents - **1 Kgs 17:1** — Elijah shuts up the heavens. - **Exod 7:17-20** — Moses turns water to blood. - **Zech 4** — Two olive trees and lampstands. ## Shared st…

from.title: Jacobs Twelve Sons
from.body: # Jacob's Twelve Sons → The Twelve Tribes and the Twelve Apostles ## OT Story — Genesis 29-30; 49 Through Leah, Rachel, and their handmaids, Jacob fathers twelve sons. In Genesis 49 he blesses each, prophesying tribal destinies. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 19:28: The twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the tribes. - Revelation 21:12-14: The New Jerusalem bears the names of the twelve tribes …

to.title: Leah Rachel Two Brides
to.body: # Leah and Rachel / Two Brides → Israel and the Church Made One ## OT Story — Genesis 29:15-30 Jacob loves Rachel but is deceived into marrying Leah first. Both bear sons to him; together with the handmaids they produce the twelve tribes. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-32: Christ loves one bride, the church. - Galatians 3:28: One people in Christ from Jew and Gentile. - Ruth 4:11: 'Rachel and …

from.title: soteria
from.body: # soteria (σωτηρία) — salvation ## Semantic field The state or event of being saved — deliverance, preservation, salvation. Noun of sozo. ## Theological weight "The gospel… is the power of God for soteria to everyone who believes" (Rom 1:16). Soteria includes justification, sanctification, and glorification — past, present, future deliverance. ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew yashua / yeshuah.…

to.title: sozo
to.body: # sozo (σῴζω) — save / heal ## Semantic field To save, rescue, heal, deliver, preserve. Used both for physical healing/rescue and for eschatological salvation. The same verb covers both. ## Theological weight "He will sozo His people from their sins" (Matt 1:21). The healings and exorcisms are sozo-events — concrete signs of total salvation. Salvation has past, present, and future tenses. ## Septu…

from.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
from.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

to.title: Revelation 14 Lamb On Zion
to.body: # Revelation 14: Lamb on Mount Zion ## NT Text — Rev 14:1-5 The Lamb stands on Mount Zion with 144,000 bearing his Father's name on their foreheads. Psalm 2's enthronement on Zion fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:6** — I have set my king on Zion, my holy hill. - **Joel 2:32** — Mount Zion shall be refuge. - **Obad 17** — On Mount Zion there shall be deliverance. ## Shared structure - Lamb ent…

from.title: Isaiah 11 Branch Of Jesse
from.body: # Branch from Jesse's Stump → Peaceable Kingdom of the Root ## OT Story — Isaiah 11:1-10 From the stump of Jesse a shoot comes; the Spirit rests on him. He judges with righteousness. The wolf dwells with the lamb. The earth is full of the knowledge of the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 15:12: 'The root of Jesse will come; in him the Gentiles will hope.' - Revelation 5:5: The Root of David has co…

to.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
to.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

from.title: elpis
from.body: # elpis (ἐλπίς) — hope ## Semantic field Hope, confident expectation. In classical Greek hope was often ambivalent (could be false); in biblical Greek it acquires confident certainty grounded in God's promise. ## Theological weight "Hope does not put us to shame" (Rom 5:5). The "blessed elpis" (Titus 2:13) is Christ's return. Hope is "the anchor of the soul" (Heb 6:19) — secure because moored in r…

to.title: tikvah
to.body: # tikvah (תִּקְוָה) — hope ## Semantic field Hope, expectation — etymologically related to "cord" (Rahab's scarlet tikvah at the window). Hope as binding-cord stretched toward future. ## Theological weight "I have a tikvah, says YHWH, plans for shalom and not for ra, to give you a future and a tikvah" (Jer 29:11). Tikvah is grounded in chesed, not optimism; Lamentations 3 yokes them. ## Septuagint…

from.title: Pauls Defense Before Agrippa
from.body: # Paul's Defense Before Agrippa ## NT Text — Acts 26 Paul tells his Damascus story before Agrippa, climaxing in 'King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets?' Almost-persuaded becomes the famous response. ## OT Antecedents - **1 Sam 26** — David before Saul. - **Jer 38** — Jeremiah before Zedekiah. - **Dan 5** — Daniel before Belshazzar. ## Shared structure - Royal audience - Prophetic appeal - Pers…

to.title: Sauls Damascus Conversion
to.body: # Saul's Damascus Conversion ## NT Text — Acts 9:1-19 Saul is blinded by a light from heaven and hears 'why do you persecute me?' The persecutor becomes apostle; Sinai-theophany shape meets prophetic call. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 3:1-6** — Burning bush call of Moses. - **Isa 6:1-8** — Isaiah's throne-vision call. - **Jer 1:4-10** — Jeremiah's prophetic call. ## Shared structure - Light from hea…

from.title: Ezekiel 10 Glory Departs
from.body: # Glory Departs the Temple → Christ Departs the Temple ## OT Story — Ezekiel 10-11 The glory of the LORD lifts from the cherubim, moves to the threshold, and finally to the mountain east of the city. The temple is left desolate before destruction. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:38-24:2: 'Your house is left to you desolate'; not one stone left. - Luke 21:5-6: Foretelling the temple's fall. - Mark 1…

to.title: Temple Dedication Glory

from.title: Cain Abel Two Sacrifices
from.body: # Cain & Abel / Two Sacrifices → Christ's Better Blood ## OT Story — Genesis 4:1-16 The first sons of Adam bring offerings. Abel offers the firstborn of his flock with their fat (a blood sacrifice); Cain offers the fruit of the ground. The LORD receives Abel's offering but not Cain's. Cain rises against his righteous brother and murders him; Abel's blood "cries from the ground." ## NT Fulfillment …

to.title: Coats Of Skin First Sacrifice
to.body: # Coats of Skin → Garments of Salvation ## OT Story — Genesis 3:21 After the fall, Adam and Eve cover themselves with fig leaves. God himself makes garments of skin and clothes them. Implicit in the act is the death of an animal — the first sacrifice and covering. ## NT Fulfillment - Isaiah 61:10: He has clothed me with the garments of salvation, covered me with the robe of righteousness. - Romans…

from.title: 2 Cor 3 Veil Lifted In Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 3: Veil Lifted in Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 3:7-18 Moses' veil over his face symbolizes the old covenant's partial reading. In Christ the veil is taken away and believers behold his glory unveiled. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 34:29-35** — Moses' face shines; he veils it. - **Jer 31:33** — Law written on the heart. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. ## Shared structure - Veil of glory …

to.title: Hebrews 8 New Covenant Better
to.body: # Hebrews 8: A Better New Covenant ## NT Text — Heb 8:6-13 Christ's ministry is more excellent because it mediates a better covenant. The author quotes Jeremiah 31 in full — laws on hearts, knowledge of the Lord, sins remembered no more. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 31:31-34** — Behold the days are coming — I will make a new covenant. - **Ezek 36:26-27** — New heart and Spirit. - **Deut 30:6** — Circ…

from.title: Ezekiels Eagles And Vine
from.body: # Ezekiel's Eagles and Vine ## What happens Ezekiel describes two great eagles and a vine: one eagle plants the vine, which then turns toward another; God will plant a tender twig that grows into a noble cedar. ## Typological significance Geopolitical allegory of Judah caught between Babylon and Egypt, ending in a messianic promise: the tender twig planted by God on the high mountain prefigures Ch…

to.title: Mustard Seed
to.body: # The Mustard Seed ## What happens The kingdom is like a mustard seed — smallest of seeds — that grows into a tree where the birds of the air nest in its branches. ## Typological significance Ezekiel's and Daniel's imperial-tree imagery applied to the kingdom of God: from negligible beginnings, a sheltering reign for the nations. Small origin, world-encompassing canopy. ## Connects to `[[../Motifs…

from.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
from.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

to.title: Veil Of Tabernacle
to.body: # Veil of the Tabernacle Torn → New and Living Way ## OT Story — Exodus 26:31-33 A heavy veil with cherubim separates the holy place from the most holy. Only the high priest enters, once a year, with blood. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:51: At Christ's death the veil is torn from top to bottom. - Hebrews 10:19-22: We enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way opened thro…

from.title: 2 Peter 3 Day As Thousand Years
from.body: # 2 Peter 3: Day as a Thousand Years ## NT Text — 2 Pet 3:8-13 With the Lord one day is as a thousand years; he is not slow but patient. New heavens and new earth are promised after dissolution by fire. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 90:4** — A thousand years in your sight as yesterday. - **Isa 65:17** — New heavens and new earth. - **Mal 4:1** — Day burning like an oven. ## Shared structure - Divine ti…

to.title: Apocalypse Of Weeks Enoch
to.body: # Apocalypse of Weeks → Periodized History and Final Age ## OT Story — 1 Enoch 93; 91:11-17 History is divided into ten 'weeks'; the seventh sees apostasy and the chosen of the eternal plant; later weeks bring judgment and new creation. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 24:14: This gospel will be preached throughout the world, and then the end. - 2 Peter 3:13: New heavens and new earth. - Galatians 4:4:…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Tree Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Tree Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Acts 5:30; Gal 3:13; Rev 22 Eden's two trees, Abraham's oaks, Israel's vine and olive, the tree of curse on which Christ hung, the tree of life in Revelation. Trees thread sin and salvation. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:9** — Tree of life and tree of knowledge. - **Gen 21:33** — Abraham's tamarisk. - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is anyone hung on a tree. - **Ezek…

from.title: Light Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Light Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1; Rev 21 Genesis's first creative word is 'let there be light'; the prophets promise light to the nations; Christ is the Light of the World; the New Jerusalem needs no lamp. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:3** — Let there be light. - **Isa 9:2** — People in darkness have seen great light. - **Isa 60:1-3** — Arise, shine. ## Shared structure - Creation li…

to.title: Wedding Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wedding Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Rev 19 From Adam's awakening to Eve to Cana's water-turned-wine to the marriage supper of the Lamb, weddings mark new-creation thresholds. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:22-24** — First wedding. - **Song 3:11** — Day of Solomon's wedding. - **Isa 62:5** — Like a bridegroom rejoices. ## Shared structure - Anticipation - Feast - Joy - Covenant initiat…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
to.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

from.title: Good Shepherd Discourse
from.body: # The Good Shepherd Discourse ## What happens Jesus contrasts the true shepherd, who enters by the door and lays down His life for the sheep, with thieves and hirelings; He declares 'I am the good shepherd.' ## Typological significance Ezekiel's promise of YHWH coming as Shepherd in person fulfilled. The good shepherd's defining mark — laying down His life for the sheep — interprets the cross as p…

to.title: True Vine Discourse
to.body: # The True Vine Discourse ## What happens Jesus declares 'I am the true vine, my Father is the vinedresser.' Branches abiding in Him bear much fruit; those that do not are pruned away and burned. ## Typological significance Israel as failed vine (Psalm 80, Isaiah 5) replaced by Christ as the true vine — believers as branches in Him. The vine metaphor reframes covenant union as organic indwelling. …

from.title: Great Commission
from.body: # Great Commission ## NT Text — Matt 28:16-20 Jesus on a Galilean mountain commissions disciples to make disciples of all nations. Daniel-7 authority enacted in mission. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — All peoples should serve him. - **Gen 12:3** — In you all nations blessed. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. ## Shared structure - Mountain - Universal authority - Universal mission - Trinita…

to.title: Sermon On Mount New Sinai
to.body: # Sermon on the Mount as New Sinai ## NT Text — Matt 5-7 Jesus ascends a mountain, sits down, and authoritatively teaches his disciples. Matthew structures the scene to evoke Moses at Sinai — but the new lawgiver speaks not 'thus says the LORD' but 'I say to you.' ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19-20** — Moses on Sinai delivers the Decalogue. - **Deut 30:11-14** — Word near you — internalized Torah an…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

to.title: Job My Redeemer Lives
to.body: # Job / 'My Redeemer Lives' → Resurrection Hope ## OT Story — Job 19:25-27 'I know that my Redeemer lives, and at the last he will stand upon the earth. After my skin has been thus destroyed, yet in my flesh I shall see God.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 11:25: I am the resurrection and the life. - 1 Corinthians 15:20: Christ has been raised, the firstfruits. - Revelation 1:18: I died, and behold I am…

from.title: Joseph Provides Bread
from.body: # Joseph Provides Bread in Famine → Christ the Bread of Life ## OT Story — Genesis 41:53-57 Famine spreads over all the earth. Joseph alone has bread. 'Go to Joseph,' Pharaoh says, 'and do what he tells you.' Nations come to Egypt to be fed. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:35: I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger. - John 2:5: Mary's 'do whatever he tells you' echoes Pharaoh's 'g…

to.title: Joseph Reveals Self
to.body: # Joseph Reveals Himself → Israel Recognizes the Pierced One ## OT Story — Genesis 45:1-15 After testing his brothers, Joseph dismisses his attendants and weeps aloud: 'I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?' The brothers are terrified, then reconciled. He sends them back to bring their father. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 7:13: Joseph 'made himself known to his brothers' — Stephen uses the verb as ty…

from.title: Paul In Rome Unhindered
from.body: # Paul in Rome, Unhindered ## NT Text — Acts 28:30-31 Acts ends with Paul preaching the kingdom in Rome 'unhindered.' Jerusalem-to-Rome arc completes; the gospel reaches the ends of the earth. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 49:6** — To the end of the earth. - **Ps 96:3** — Declare his glory among the nations. - **Dan 7:14** — All nations should serve him. ## Shared structure - Capital reached - Boldnes…

to.title: Pauls Defense Before Agrippa
to.body: # Paul's Defense Before Agrippa ## NT Text — Acts 26 Paul tells his Damascus story before Agrippa, climaxing in 'King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets?' Almost-persuaded becomes the famous response. ## OT Antecedents - **1 Sam 26** — David before Saul. - **Jer 38** — Jeremiah before Zedekiah. - **Dan 5** — Daniel before Belshazzar. ## Shared structure - Royal audience - Prophetic appeal - Pers…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

to.title: 2 Peter 1 Make Calling Sure
to.body: # 2 Peter 1: Make Your Calling Sure ## NT Text — 2 Pet 1:3-11 Add to your faith virtue, knowledge, self-control, perseverance, godliness, brotherly affection, love. By these qualities you confirm your calling and election. ## OT Antecedents - **Prov 1:7** — Beginning of knowledge. - **Ps 15** — Who shall dwell on your holy hill? - **Mic 6:8** — Walk humbly with God. ## Shared structure - Faith as …

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Born Anew
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Born Anew through Living Word ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:22-25 Born again through the living and abiding word of God — Isaiah's 'grass withers, the word stands forever' cited as charter. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 40:6-8** — Grass withers, word stands forever. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. - **Ps 119:25** — Give me life according to your word. ## Shared structure - New birth - Liv…

to.title: 1 Peter 3 Baptism Like Flood
to.body: # 1 Peter 3: Baptism Like the Flood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 3:18-22 Eight souls were saved through water in Noah's ark; baptism corresponds to this. The flood typifies the cleansing-and-deliverance of Christian baptism. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 7-8** — Noah's flood and ark. - **Gen 6:5-8** — Wickedness met by judgment. - **Ezek 14:14** — Noah, Daniel, and Job — paradigmatic righteous. ## Shared struct…

from.title: Elijah Mount Carmel
from.body: # Elijah on Carmel → Fire from Heaven Vindicates the True God ## OT Story — 1 Kings 18:20-40 Elijah challenges Baal's prophets at Carmel. They cry all day; nothing. Elijah drenches his altar, prays, and fire falls — consuming sacrifice, water, and stones. The people fall on their faces: 'The LORD, he is God!' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:3: Tongues of fire descend. - James 5:17-18: Elijah was a man …

to.title: Malachi Refiners Fire
to.body: # Malachi 3 / Refiner's Fire → Trials Purifying the Saints ## OT Story — Malachi 3:2-4 'Who can endure the day of his coming? For he is like a refiner's fire and like fullers' soap… He will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver.' ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 1:6-7: Tested by fire — your faith more precious than gold. - 1 Corinthians 3:12-15: Each one's work tested by fire. - …

from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Psalm 110 Priest King
to.body: # Psalm 110 / 'Sit at My Right Hand' → Priest-King Forever ## OT Story — Psalm 110 'The LORD says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool… You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 22:41-46: Jesus' messianic riddle from this psalm. - Hebrews 5:6; 7:1-28: Christ's eternal priesthood after Melchizedek. - Acts 2:34-36: Pete…

from.title: Calling Of Twelve
from.body: # Calling of the Twelve ## NT Text — Mark 3:13-19; Luke 6:12-16 Jesus ascends a mountain and appoints twelve apostles. The number is constitutive: a new Israel is being formed around a new lawgiver. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49** — Twelve patriarchs. - **Exod 24:4** — Twelve pillars at Sinai. - **Josh 4:1-9** — Twelve stones from the Jordan. ## Shared structure - Twelve representatives - Mountain …

to.title: Great Commission
to.body: # Great Commission ## NT Text — Matt 28:16-20 Jesus on a Galilean mountain commissions disciples to make disciples of all nations. Daniel-7 authority enacted in mission. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — All peoples should serve him. - **Gen 12:3** — In you all nations blessed. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. ## Shared structure - Mountain - Universal authority - Universal mission - Trinita…

from.title: Bitter Water Sweetened By Wood
from.body: # Bitter Waters of Marah Sweetened by Wood → Cross Sweetens Life ## OT Story — Exodus 15:22-27 Three days from the sea, Israel finds water but it is bitter. They cry; Moses throws a tree into the water and it becomes sweet. The LORD reveals himself as YHWH-Rapha, the healer. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 2:24: By his wounds you have been healed. - Galatians 3:13: Christ became a curse, hanging on a …

to.title: Words From Cross I Thirst
to.body: # Word from Cross: I Thirst ## NT Text — John 19:28 Jesus says 'I thirst' to fulfill Scripture. The Bread-of-Life is consumed by thirst that he might quench ours. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Vinegar for my thirst. - **Ps 22:15** — Tongue cleaves. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul makes offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Thirst expressed - Sour wine offered - Fulfillment formula - Reversal ## Theo…

from.title: Apocalypse Of Weeks Enoch
from.body: # Apocalypse of Weeks → Periodized History and Final Age ## OT Story — 1 Enoch 93; 91:11-17 History is divided into ten 'weeks'; the seventh sees apostasy and the chosen of the eternal plant; later weeks bring judgment and new creation. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 24:14: This gospel will be preached throughout the world, and then the end. - 2 Peter 3:13: New heavens and new earth. - Galatians 4:4:…

to.title: Revelation 20 Millennium
to.body: # Revelation 20: The Millennium ## NT Text — Rev 20:1-6 The dragon is bound; the saints reign with Christ a thousand years. A debated vision of the church-age or future kingdom. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 90:4** — Thousand years as one day. - **Isa 65:18-25** — Long-lived peaceful kingdom. - **Ezek 37:24-28** — David shepherd-king of restored Israel. ## Shared structure - Dragon bound - First resurr…

from.title: Flight To Egypt
from.body: # Flight to Egypt ## NT Text — Matt 2:13-15 Joseph takes Mary and the child to Egypt to escape Herod. Israel's history is recapitulated: the Son goes down to Egypt as Israel did, in order to be called out as Israel was. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 46** — Jacob's family goes down to Egypt for refuge. - **Exod 4:19** — Moses told 'all who sought your life are dead' — quoted in Matt 2:20. - **Hos 11:1*…

to.title: Forty Days In Wilderness
to.body: # Forty Days in the Wilderness ## NT Text — Matt 4:1-2; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-2 Jesus fasts forty days in the wilderness, recapitulating Israel's forty years, Moses' two forty-day fasts on Sinai, and Elijah's forty-day journey to Horeb. Where Israel failed, the Son succeeds. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 24:18; 34:28** — Moses forty days on Sinai. - **Num 14:33-34** — Israel forty years in the wilde…

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: Hebrews 9 Once For All
to.body: # Hebrews 9: Once for All ## NT Text — Heb 9:11-14, 24-28 Christ entered the heavenly sanctuary once for all by his own blood. Annual atonement is superseded by single, eternal redemption. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement — annual. - **Exod 26:33** — Veil before the most-holy. - **Zech 9:11** — Blood of the covenant. ## Shared structure - Heavenly sanctuary - Once-for-all entry - …

from.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
from.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

to.title: Revelation 13 Mark 666
to.body: # Revelation 13: Mark and 666 ## NT Text — Rev 13:16-18 All are forced to receive a mark — number of a man, six hundred sixty-six. Counter-seal to the saints' marking; failure-of-seven trebled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 9:4** — Mark on foreheads of the faithful. - **Dan 3:5-7** — Forced worship of image. - **1 Kgs 10:14** — Solomon's annual gold: 666 talents — kingdom-pride. ## Shared structure -…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

from.title: Lamentations Suffering City
from.body: # Lamentations / Weeping Over the City → Christ's Tears Over Jerusalem ## OT Story — Lamentations 1-3 Jeremiah laments the fallen city: 'How lonely sits the city that was full of people… Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by?' He bears the people's sorrow. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:41-44: Jesus wept over Jerusalem. - Matthew 23:37-39: Lament over Jerusalem. - Lamentations 3:22-23: His mercie…

to.title: Revelation 18 Babylon Fallen
to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

from.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

to.title: Serpent Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Serpent Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 3:14; Rev 12, 20 The serpent of Eden, the bronze serpent in the wilderness, the dragons of Daniel and the Psalms, the dragon-serpent of Revelation finally bound. The serpent's head is crushed. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:1-15** — Serpent in the garden. - **Num 21:8-9** — Bronze serpent lifted up. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1**…

from.title: Baptism Of Jesus Theophany
from.body: # Baptism of Jesus as Theophany ## NT Text — Matt 3:13-17; Mark 1:9-11; Luke 3:21-22 Jesus is baptized in the Jordan, the heavens open, the Spirit descends, and the Father speaks. The scene gathers OT theophanies and inaugurates the messianic ministry. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over the waters — new-creation echo. - **Ps 2:7** — You are my Son — coronation formula. - **Isa …

to.title: Romans 6 Baptized Into Death
to.body: # Romans 6: Baptized into His Death ## NT Text — Rom 6:1-11 Believers are baptized into Christ's death and raised to walk in newness of life. Baptism is union with the Death-and-Resurrection of Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage as death/resurrection of Israel. - **Josh 3** — Jordan crossing into the land. - **Ezek 36:25-27** — Sprinkled with clean water; new spirit. ## Shar…

from.title: Pierced Side Blood And Water
from.body: # Pierced Side: Blood and Water ## NT Text — John 19:34-37 A soldier pierces Jesus' side; blood and water flow. John cites Zechariah 12:10. ## OT Antecedents - **Zech 12:10** — They will look on him whom they pierced. - **Exod 17:6** — Water from struck rock. - **Ezek 47:1** — Water from temple. ## Shared structure - Pierced side - Blood and water - Look-and-mourn - Temple-water echo ## Theologica…

to.title: Sweat As Blood
to.body: # Sweat as Blood ## NT Text — Luke 22:44 Luke records that Jesus' sweat became 'like great drops of blood' in Gethsemane. The detail echoes Adam's curse of toilsome sweat and anticipates the cross's bloodshed; the new Adam labors in the garden of obedience. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:19** — By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread. - **Lev 17:11** — Life is in the blood. - **Ps 22:14** — I a…

from.title: Psalm 118 Hosanna
from.body: # Psalm 118 / Hosanna → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Psalm 118:25-26 'Save us, we pray, O LORD! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD!' Sung at pilgrimage feasts as Israel ascends to Jerusalem. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:9: 'Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes…' - John 12:13: Palm branches and the same cry. - Matthew 23:39: 'You will not see me again until you say…'…

to.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
to.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

from.title: Bones Of Joseph Carried Out
from.body: # Bones of Joseph Carried Out → Hope of Resurrection ## OT Story — Genesis 50:24-26 Dying in Egypt, Joseph commands: 'God will surely visit you; carry up my bones from here.' Moses takes the bones at the exodus; Joshua buries them in the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:22: By faith Joseph spoke of the exodus and gave instructions concerning his bones. - Ezekiel 37: Dry bones live by the Spiri…

to.title: Joseph In The Pit
to.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Living Stones
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. Psalm 118 and Isaiah 28 quoted. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 28:16** — Cornerstone in Zion — quoted. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected — quoted. - **Dan 2:34** — Stone cut without hands. ## Shared structure - Cornerstone - Livi…

to.title: 1 Peter 5 Chief Shepherd
to.body: # 1 Peter 5: Chief Shepherd ## NT Text — 1 Pet 5:1-4 Elders shepherd the flock; when the Chief Shepherd appears, they will receive the unfading crown of glory. Davidic-shepherd typology gathered into Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 34:23** — My servant David — one shepherd. - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Zech 11:7-17** — Two shepherd-staffs. ## Shared structure - Under-shepherds - C…

from.title: Daniel 9 Seventy Weeks
from.body: # Seventy Weeks of Daniel → The Time of Messiah ## OT Story — Daniel 9:24-27 Seventy weeks are decreed to finish transgression, atone for iniquity, and bring everlasting righteousness. from.title: Boaz Foot Of Cross Threshing
from.body: # Threshing Floor of Boaz → Approaching the Redeemer in Boldness ## OT Story — Ruth 3 At Naomi's instruction, Ruth comes by night to the threshing floor where Boaz sleeps. She lies at his feet and asks him to spread his wing over her. He responds with covenant kindness. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:16: Draw near with boldness to the throne of grace. - Romans 5:8: While we were yet sinners, Christ…

to.title: Rahab Scarlet Cord
to.body: # Rahab's Scarlet Cord → Blood-Marked Household Saved ## OT Story — Joshua 2; 6:22-25 Rahab the Canaanite prostitute hides the spies and asks for mercy. They give her a scarlet cord to hang from her window; only those in her house with the cord will be spared when Jericho falls. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:31: By faith Rahab did not perish. - James 2:25: She was justified by works that demonstr…

from.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
from.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire → God Tabernacling With Us ## OT Story — Exodus 13:21-22; 40:34-38 The LORD goes before Israel as a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night. The pillar fills the finished tabernacle. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled among us; we beheld his glory. - Matthew 17:5: A bright cloud overshadows; 'this is my beloved Son.' - Revelation 21:23:…

to.title: Tongues Of Fire
to.body: # Tongues of Fire ## NT Text — Acts 2:3-4 Divided tongues 'as of fire' rest on each disciple. The Sinai-fire that descended on the mountain now rests on the people, individualizing the Shekinah. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:18** — Sinai descended in fire. - **Exod 3:2** — Burning bush — fire that does not consume. - **Mal 3:2** — Like a refiner's fire. ## Shared structure - Divine fire - Resting …

from.title: Jacobs Ladder
from.body: # Jacob's Ladder → Jesus as the Ladder (His Own Citation) ## OT Story — Genesis 28:10-22 Jacob is fleeing his brother Esau, who wants to kill him for the stolen blessing. Alone, in the open, he stops *"because the sun had set"*. He takes a stone for a pillow and lies down. He dreams: *"behold, a **ladder** [*sullam* — more accurately a stairway/ramp] set up on the earth, and the **top of it reache…

to.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
to.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

to.title: Hebrews 2 Tasted Death For Everyone
to.body: # Hebrews 2: Tasted Death for Everyone ## NT Text — Heb 2:9-18 Jesus, made lower than angels for a little while, tasted death for everyone. He is a merciful and faithful high priest because he shared in our flesh. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 8** — Made lower than the heavenly beings. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear iniquities. - **Job 19:25** — Redeemer who stands. ## Shared structure - Incarnation as solidar…

from.title: James Martyred
from.body: # James Son of Zebedee Martyred ## NT Text — Acts 12:1-2 Herod kills James with the sword. The first apostolic martyr fulfills Jesus' prophecy that the sons of Zebedee would drink his cup. ## OT Antecedents - **1 Kgs 19:10** — Slain prophets of YHWH. - **2 Chr 24:21** — Stoned prophet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Royal hostility - Sword execution - Fulfillment of J…

to.title: Stephens Martyrdom Like Christ
to.body: # Stephen's Martyrdom Patterned on Christ ## NT Text — Acts 7:54-60 Stephen prays 'receive my spirit' and 'do not hold this sin against them' — directly echoing the cross-words of Jesus. The first Christian martyr dies like his Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **2 Chr 24:20-22** — Zechariah son of Jehoiad…

from.title: Shepherd Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Shepherd Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 10; Heb 13:20 Abel, Jacob, Moses, David — all shepherds. YHWH as Shepherd in Psalm 23 and Ezekiel 34. Christ as Good Shepherd who lays down his life. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Ezek 34** — Faithless shepherds vs. true. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Pastoral vocation - Sheep-guidance -…

to.title: Vine Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Vine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 15 Israel as vine in Psalm 80 and Isaiah 5 — disappointing fruit. Christ as the True Vine, with believers as branches abiding in him. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 80:8-16** — Vine brought out of Egypt. - **Isa 5:1-7** — Vineyard of the LORD. - **Jer 2:21** — Choice vine turned degenerate. - **Hos 10:1** — Israel a luxuriant vine. ## Shared structure - Plan…

from.title: Seventy Weeks Daniel 9
from.body: # Seventy Weeks (Daniel 9) ## The oracle Seventy weeks are decreed upon Daniel's people to finish transgression, atone for iniquity, bring everlasting righteousness, seal vision, and anoint a most holy place; after sixty-nine weeks Messiah will be cut off. ## Date / context Given to Daniel in Babylon, c. 539 BC, during his prayer over Jeremiah's seventy-year prophecy. ## Fulfillment Christian inte…

to.title: Time Times Half Time
to.body: # Time, Times, and Half a Time ## The oracle The persecution of the saints is limited to "a time, times, and half a time" — a fixed and bounded period of tribulation. ## Date / context Danielic visions, 6th c. BC. ## Fulfillment Revelation reuses the formula (12:14) alongside parallel measures (forty-two months, 1260 days) to describe the church's wilderness period under satanic assault. ## Connec…

from.title: 1 John 4 God Is Love
from.body: # 1 John 4: God Is Love ## NT Text — 1 John 4:7-12 Love is from God; whoever loves has been born of God. In this the love of God was manifested: he sent his only Son as propitiation for our sins. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 7:7-8** — God's electing love. - **Hos 11:1-4** — Love drawing Israel. - **Ps 36:5-7** — Steadfast love of the LORD. ## Shared structure - God-as-love - Love manifested in sendi…

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
to.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

from.title: Absalom Rebellion
from.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

to.title: David Spares Saul
to.body: # David Spares Saul → Christ Forbearing With Persecutors ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 24, 26 Twice David could kill Saul who is hunting him. He refuses: 'I will not put forth my hand against the LORD's anointed.' He waits on God to vindicate. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 12:17-21: Do not avenge yourselves; leave it to the wrath of God. - 1 Peter 2:23: When reviled, he did not revile; he entrusted himself …

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Trial Before Pilate Innocent Verdict
to.body: # Trial Before Pilate: Innocent Verdict ## NT Text — John 18:28-19:16 Pilate repeatedly declares Jesus innocent yet hands him over. The pattern matches the Servant unjustly condemned and the Passover lamb inspected and found without blemish. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:8-9** — Cut off though no violence in him. - **Exod 12:5** — Lamb without blemish. - **Ps 2:1-2** — Rulers gather against the LOR…

from.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
from.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

to.title: Plague Of Firstborn Spared By Blood
to.body: # Plague of the Firstborn / Blood on the Door → Christ's Blood Spares ## OT Story — Exodus 12:21-30 Israel slaughters the Passover lamb, strikes its blood on the doorposts, and stays inside. The destroyer passes over houses marked by blood. Egypt's firstborn die. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 5:7: Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed. - 1 Peter 1:18-19: Redeemed by the precious blood o…

from.title: Revelation 19 Word Of God On White Horse
from.body: # Revelation 19: Word of God on a White Horse ## NT Text — Rev 19:11-16 Heaven opens; the rider on the white horse is called Faithful and True, the Word of God. He treads the winepress of God's wrath. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 63:1-6** — Treading the winepress of wrath. - **Ps 2:9** — Rule with iron rod. - **Zech 14:3-4** — LORD goes forth to fight. ## Shared structure - Heaven opened - Faithful-T…

to.title: Revelation 5 Scroll And Seals
to.body: # Revelation 5: Scroll and Seven Seals ## NT Text — Rev 5:1-7 A scroll sealed with seven seals — none worthy to open. The Lamb takes it. Daniel's sealed words and Ezekiel's scroll converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 2:9-3:3** — Scroll given to the prophet. - **Dan 12:4** — Seal the book until the time of the end. - **Isa 29:11** — Words of a sealed book. ## Shared structure - Sealed scroll - Sear…

from.title: Jeremiah 31 New Covenant
from.body: # Jeremiah 31 / New Covenant → Christ's Blood Inaugurates It ## OT Story — Jeremiah 31:31-34 'Behold, the days are coming when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel… I will put my law within them, write it on their hearts, and forgive their iniquity.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 22:20: This cup is the new covenant in my blood. - Hebrews 8:6-13: Christ's covenant is established on better…

to.body: # Veil of the Tabernacle Torn → New and Living Way ## OT Story — Exodus 26:31-33 A heavy veil with cherubim separates the holy place from the most holy. Only the high priest enters, once a year, with blood. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:51: At Christ's death the veil is torn from top to bottom. - Hebrews 10:19-22: We enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way opened thro…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Living Stones
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. to.body: # Aaron's Breastplate of Twelve Stones → Christ Carries His People on His Heart ## OT Story — Exodus 28:15-30 The high priest's breastplate bears twelve gemstones, each engraved with a tribe's name, worn over his heart whenever he enters the holy place. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:14-16: A great high priest who sympathizes — boldness to draw near. - Hebrews 7:25: He always lives to intercede. - …

from.body: # Throne Chariot / Merkavah → Revelation's Throne Vision ## OT Story — Ezekiel 1 Ezekiel sees a stormy theophany — four living creatures, wheels within wheels, a throne of sapphire, a figure of fire and rainbow. to.body: # 2 Peter 1: Make Your Calling Sure ## NT Text — 2 Pet 1:3-11 Add to your faith virtue, knowledge, self-control, perseverance, godliness, brotherly affection, love. from.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.title: Revelation 7 144000 Sealed
to.body: # Revelation 7: 144,000 Sealed ## NT Text — Rev 7:1-8 A great seal placed on the foreheads of 144,000 from all the tribes of Israel. Ezekiel's marking of the faithful is echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 9:4-6** — Mark on the foreheads of those who sigh. - **Num 1** — Tribal census. - **Isa 49:6** — Restoration of preserved Israel. ## Shared structure - Mark on foreheads - Tribal listing - Preser…

from.title: Psalm 22 Crucifixion
from.body: # Psalm 22 → The Crucifixion ## OT Story — Psalm 22 A Davidic lament that pivots from extreme suffering to vindicated praise. The opening cry — *"My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"* (22:1) — gives way to a sequence of concrete tortures: - *"All who see me mock me; they make mouths at me, they wag their heads"* (22:7) - *"He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him!"* (22:8) — the mockers' t…

to.title: Samson Solo Victory In Death
to.body: # Samson's Death Brings Greater Victory → Christ Triumphs by Dying ## OT Story — Judges 16:23-31 Blinded, mocked, between two pillars, Samson prays once more. He pushes; the temple collapses; he kills more in his death than in his life. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:14-15: Through death he destroyed the one who has the power of death. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them…

from.title: David Anointed Youngest
from.body: # David Anointed in Bethlehem → Christ Anointed ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 16:1-13 Samuel comes to Bethlehem. Jesse parades seven sons; God chooses none. David, the youngest, is summoned from the sheep. Samuel anoints him; the Spirit rushes upon him. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:22: God raised David to be king, a man after his own heart. - Luke 4:18: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; he has anointed m…

to.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
to.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. The LORD laughs; he installs his King on Zion. 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

from.title: Hebrews 11 Cloud Of Witnesses
from.body: # Hebrews 11: Cloud of Witnesses ## NT Text — Heb 11:1-12:1 A roll-call of faith — Abel, Enoch, Noah, Abraham, Sarah, Moses, Rahab, and more — culminates in 'such a great cloud of witnesses.' Faith looks forward to the same promise we possess. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4-25** — Patriarchal exemplars. - **Exod** — Mosaic figures. - **2 Kgs 2** — Elijah taken up. ## Shared structure - Definition of …

to.title: Spies Twelve
to.body: # Twelve Spies / Two Faithful → Witnesses of the Promise ## OT Story — Numbers 13 Twelve spies survey Canaan. Ten report giants; two — Caleb and Joshua — say, 'We can surely go up.' Israel believes the ten and forfeits the land for forty years. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 3:16-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 11:30-31: Faith brought down Jericho. - Luke 10:1: Two witnesses s…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Manna In Wilderness
to.body: # Manna in the Wilderness → Bread of Life ## OT Story — Exodus 16 God rains bread from heaven; Israel gathers a day's portion. None on the Sabbath. It must be eaten daily; hoarded manna spoils. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:31-35: 'I am the bread of life' — Jesus contrasts manna and himself. - Revelation 2:17: To the one who conquers I will give hidden manna. - Deut 8:3 / Matt 4:4: Man lives by every…

from.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
from.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

to.title: Hebrews 7 Forever Priest
to.body: # Hebrews 7: A Priest Forever ## NT Text — Heb 7:23-28 Christ holds his priesthood permanently because he continues forever. He is able to save to the uttermost because he always lives to intercede. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever. - **Lev 16** — Annual atonement — repeated. - **Num 25:13** — Phinehas's perpetual priesthood — partial type. ## Shared structure - Eternal priesthood…

from.title: Serpent Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Serpent Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 3:14; Rev 12, 20 The serpent of Eden, the bronze serpent in the wilderness, the dragons of Daniel and the Psalms, the dragon-serpent of Revelation finally bound. The serpent's head is crushed. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:1-15** — Serpent in the garden. - **Num 21:8-9** — Bronze serpent lifted up. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1**…

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: elah
from.body: # elah (אֱלָהּ) — God ## Semantic field Aramaic for "god / God" — cognate with Hebrew El/Elohim. The standard divine title in the Aramaic OT sections. ## Theological weight Daniel speaks of "Elah of heaven" — the universal God acknowledged even within pagan imperial settings. Jesus' cry from the cross — "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani" (Mark 15:34) — uses the Aramaic form, quoting Psalm 22. ## Septu…

to.title: elohim
to.body: # elohim (אֱלֹהִים) — God / gods ## Semantic field Plural in form, singular in reference when used of YHWH. Also refers to other heavenly beings (Ps 82) and rarely to powerful humans (Ex 7:1). The plural form with singular verbs is grammatically distinctive. ## Theological weight The opening word-pair of Scripture: "In the beginning Elohim created." Names the transcendent Creator-God. The plural i…

from.title: Philippians 2 Christ Hymn Kenosis
from.body: # Philippians 2: Christ Hymn Kenosis ## NT Text — Phil 2:5-11 Christ, though in the form of God, emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, becoming obedient to death — even death on a cross — and was exalted to receive every knee's bow. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 45:23** — Every knee shall bow to me — quoted. - **Isa 52:13-53:12** — Servant abased and exalted. - **Dan 7:13-14** — Universal dom…

from.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
from.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. to.title: Bronze Serpent
to.body: # The Bronze Serpent → "The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up" One of the typologies **Jesus himself names explicitly**. As with Jonah (Mt 12:40), Jesus' own use of this OT story authorizes its typological reading and tells us exactly how he wants it understood. ## OT Story — Numbers 21:4-9 Israel grumbles against God during the wilderness journey. God sends "fiery serpents" whose bite kills many. The …

from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…
